## Summary

Adds a minimum-1-per-criterion recommendation floor to the Pass 3 synthesis prompt (v20), fixing the bug where high-scoring criteria (e.g. concept at 8/10 in The Awakening) end up with empty `recommendations: []` because GATE 6 suppressed all their recs. Includes anti-hallucination guard — the model must NOT fabricate recs to fill the slot.

## Scope

Pass selection:

- [ ] Pass 1
- [ ] Pass 2
- [x] Pass 3

Changed files:

- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/prompts/pass3-synthesis.ts` — added MINIMUM-1-PER-CRITERION FLOOR rule after GATE 6, bumped version to v20
- `__tests__/lib/evaluation/pipeline/pass3-rec-contract-canary.test.ts` — updated version assertion

Out of scope:

- No runtime pipeline changes, no reducer changes, no UI changes
- No changes to GATE 1–6 logic itself
- No changes to scoring or confidence calculation

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

Process Change: no

## Contract Integrity

- The 7-part REC CONTRACT is unchanged
- GATE 1–6 suppression rules are unchanged
- New floor rule adds a safety net after all gates, not before
- `floor_exemption` field is additive — existing consumers ignore unknown fields

## Behavioral Quality

This PR is not reducing intelligence.

The floor rule improves output quality by ensuring every improvable criterion provides actionable feedback for the Revise workbench. The anti-hallucination guard prevents fabricated recs:

**Floor logic:**
- Score 10/10 → no rec needed (ceiling) → `floor_exemption`
- Structurally inapplicable (e.g. dialogue in zero-dialogue novel) → `floor_exemption`
- No evidence-grounded rec possible → `floor_exemption` (do NOT fabricate)
- Genuine evidence-grounded rec was gate-suppressed → generate refinement rec (must pass full 7-part contract)

## Quality Gate / Anomalies

QG_EDITORIAL_GENERIC_FEEDBACK: no QG_ behavior changes. Floor-generated recs must still pass all 7-part contract checks.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- The floor rule is prompt-level guidance only — the LLM may still occasionally produce empty arrays. The `floor_exemption` escape makes this acceptable and explicit.
- Existing artifacts are unaffected — the floor only applies to future evaluations.
- Anti-hallucination guard explicitly prohibits fabricating evidence to fill the slot.
